name: State Highway Patrol Federal Credit Union
description_short: Federal credit union serving law enforcement officers with checking, savings, loans, and mortgages. Offers specialized debt cancellation for members killed in the line of duty.
description_long:
State Highway Patrol Federal Credit Union (SHPFCU) is a federally chartered credit union with routing number 244077899, primarily serving members of the state highway patrol and law enforcement community. The institution provides traditional financial services including deposit accounts, lending products, and online banking access. SHPFCU operates with a clear mission to serve sworn law enforcement officers and their families, as evidenced by their specialized membership focus and officer-specific benefits.

The credit union offers a range of core banking and lending products. Members can access online and mobile banking for account management, fund transfers, bill payment, and ATM/shared branch locator services. Loan products include auto loans and mortgages. Deposit products feature a standard savings option plus a unique Save to Win savings account that enters members into cash prize drawings based on account balance growth ($25 increments). The institution also offers credit cards and has partnered with GreenPath Financial Wellness to provide free financial counseling services.

SHPFCU differentiates itself primarily through its law enforcement officer membership base and exclusive benefits tied to that community. Most notably, they offer "Killed in the Line of Duty" coverage—a special debt cancellation benefit available at no cost to members who serve as sworn law enforcement officers. This specialized product creates meaningful value for their target demographic. The Save to Win program also represents a distinctive savings incentive approach less common among traditional financial institutions.

As a credit union, SHPFCU offers member-owned structure and competitive rates typical of the credit union model. However, the website provides limited detail on specific rates, fees, or comprehensive product terms. The institution's geographic service area is not explicitly stated beyond the Ohio area code phone numbers (614 and 800-282 prefixes). For consumers outside the law enforcement community or without eligibility to join, membership accessibility may be limited by field-of-membership restrictions typical of occupational credit unions. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.

pricing.guarantee_details: Contact provider for current pricing and guarantee details.

pros: Specialized "Killed in the Line of Duty" debt cancellation benefit exclusive to sworn law enforcement officers at no cost
Save to Win savings account with cash prize drawings—unique incentive structure for savers
Free financial counseling through GreenPath Financial Wellness partnership
Online and mobile banking with bill pay, fund transfers, and shared branching network access
Full-service credit union offering auto loans, mortgages, and credit cards
No-surcharge ATM network access for members
Member-owned credit union structure typically offering competitive rates
cons: Membership appears restricted to law enforcement officers—eligibility requirements not fully detailed on website
Limited transparency on specific interest rates, APRs, and fee structures
Geographic service area unclear; appears Ohio-focused but not explicitly stated
Minimal product detail provided on website—requires contacting loan officers for specific terms
No information about minimum deposit requirements or account opening process
best_for: Active and retired state highway patrol officers and sworn law enforcement officers
Law enforcement families seeking law-enforcement-specific financial products and benefits
Savers interested in prize-linked savings accounts as a savings incentive tool
diagnosis: SHPFCU is best for sworn law enforcement officers and their families seeking a member-owned financial institution with specialized benefits like line-of-duty debt protection. The primary caveat is that membership appears restricted to law enforcement occupational groups, making it inaccessible to the general public, and the website lacks detailed rate and fee information necessary for comprehensive comparison.
services: Online banking with bill pay and fund transfers
Save to Win savings account with prize drawings
Killed in the Line of Duty debt cancellation coverage for sworn officers
Free financial counseling via GreenPath Financial Wellness
typical_results_timeline: Results vary by individual situation. Contact the provider to discuss expected timelines for your specific needs.
address: Mettler Toledo, 1900 Polaris Pkwy #400, Columbus, OH 43240
